content: We can frame a Neural Turing Machine as an artificial biological agent whose external memory matrix acts as a functional analog to a biological cell's heritable epigenetic markers, where the NTM's read/write heads modify persistent memory states to produce adaptive, context-dependent action in the world just as biological epigenetic agents modify heritable cellular traits to produce context-dependent functional effects. This allows us to treat the NTM's memory update dynamics as a model for how autonomous biological agents evolve adaptive behavior without altering their core genetic 'network weights'.
